patch.value: Prune src/cli/commands/index.ts barrel dead re-exports and adopt knip as a dead-export gate

2026-07-04 code-quality sweep: the commands barrel re-exports ~60 runtime symbols + ~80 types that no consumer imports via the barrel (package.json exports never exposes it; only ., ./sdk*, ./cli). knip reports 115 unused exports + 239 unused exported types repo-wide (SDK public surface needs an ignore-list). One-time sweep confirmed+fixed 2026-07-04: 57 dead loader.ts imports, 7 dead export keywords (calendar/list/http-client/fix-planning/doctor). Adopt knip (or ts-prune) with an SDK allowlist as a CI gate so the class stays extinct.
Dead re-exports pruned from the commands barrel; knip runs in static gates with a zero-findings baseline.

## Context
2026-07-04 code-quality sweep: src/cli/commands/index.ts re-exports ~60 runtime symbols + ~80 types that nothing imports via the barrel (package.json exports only exposes ., ./sdk*, ./cli). knip reports 115 unused exports + 239 unused exported types repo-wide. A one-time manual sweep on 2026-07-04 already removed 57 dead loader.ts imports and 7 dead export keywords — this item is about keeping the class extinct.

## Approach
1. Prune the commands barrel to what's actually consumed (grep each symbol; the SDK/public surface is exempt).
2. Adopt knip (preferred over ts-prune) with an allowlist for the intentional SDK public surface, wired as a CI static gate alongside the existing suppression/LOC gates.
Risk to watch: the SDK allowlist must be curated, not blanket — otherwise the gate is decorative.

## Definition of done
Barrel exports only consumed symbols; knip gate green in CI and failing on a seeded dead export; allowlist documented (why each entry is public surface).

author: maintainer-agent
message: Successor: fb3i removed one dead export; this task generalizes with a knip dead-export gate
